channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Maina Kageni Faces Loss After Arsenal Wins Premier League
item.description: Radio personality Maina Kageni is counting losses after a bet he made on Arsenal backfired. The Classic 105 host had pledged to pay KSh 1 million should Arsenal win the Premier League, ending their 22...

item.title: Sugarcane growers unite to rescue Tongaat Hulett and protect local economy
item.description: As Tongaat Hulett faces a looming liquidation hearing, a coalition of sugarcane growers has formed GrowerCo, an initiative aimed at securing the company’s financial stability and safeguarding South Af...

item.title: Satirical Youth Campaign Account Blocked on X, Founder Criticises Government Action
item.description: The X account of the satirical political campaign Cockroach Janta Party has been blocked in India following a legal request. The campaign was launched by Abhijeet Dipke, a 30-year-old political commun...

item.title: Canadian Armed Forces request return of non-essential field gear to address equipment shortages
item.description: The Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) are asking personnel in the National Capital Region to return certain field equipment, including vests for body armour and backpacks, due to critical shortages. An e-ma...

item.title: Rosenberg Children Confront Parents’ Execution and Life After Orphanhood
item.description: On June 19, 1953, Michael (10) and Robert (6) Rosenberg faced the grim news that their parents, Julius and Ethel Rosenberg, were scheduled to be executed the following day for conspiracy to commit esp...

item.title: Early Career Portraits of Ida Lupino Highlighting Her Role in ‘Come On, Marines!’ (1934)
item.description: In 1934, 16-year-old British-born actress Ida Lupino starred as Esther Smith-Hamilton in the pre-Code comedy-adventure “Come On, Marines!” produced by Paramount Pictures. The film follows a squad of U...

item.title: US Charges Former Cuban President Raúl Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
item.description: The United States Department of Justice has formally indicted Raúl Castro, the 94-year-old former president and long-time defence minister of Cuba, over his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two civ...

item.title: Ford’s 1960s “Tot-Guard”: An Early Attempt at Child Car Safety
item.description: In the late 1960s, Ford Motor Company introduced the “Tot-Guard,” an innovative child safety car seat designed to keep young children secured during car rides. Weighing only five pounds, the Tot-Guard...

item.title: IFP Mayoral Candidate Outlines Plans to Address Governance and Corruption Challenges in Johannesburg
item.description: As Johannesburg prepares for what is anticipated to be a highly competitive municipal election, Inkatha Freedom Party (IFP) mayoral candidate Mlungisi Mabaso has highlighted that the city's challenges...

item.title: South Africa Begins Roll-Out of Twice-Yearly HIV Prevention Injection Amid Funding Challenges
item.description: On June 5, South Africa will start rolling out lenacapavir (LEN), an HIV prevention injection administered only twice a year, marking a major advance in HIV prevention. LEN is particularly promising f...

item.title: MIT Study Finds Cysteine Promotes Intestinal Stem Cell Regeneration
item.description: Researchers at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology have identified cysteine, a naturally occurring amino acid present in protein-rich foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts, as a powerful stimu...

item.title: Kericho Student Resumes School After Community Support and Sponsorship
item.description: Victor Kipkirui, a high school student from Waldai Ward in Kericho County, was forced to drop out of school because his family could not afford the fees. His story caught the attention of the communit...

item.title: UK Regulator Criticizes TikTok and YouTube for Insufficient Child Safety Measures
item.description: The UK media regulator Ofcom reported that TikTok and YouTube have not implemented meaningful new measures to protect children from harmful online content, despite evidence showing that recommendation...

item.title: Aga Khan emphasises continued partnership with Pakistan in human development initiatives
item.description: Prince Rahim al-Hussaini Aga Khan V, during his first visit to Pakistan since becoming the spiritual head of the Ismaili community, met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif for a breakfast meeting where he e...

item.title: South Africa Sees Inflation Rise to 20-Month Peak Amid Fuel Price Pressures
item.description: In April 2026, South Africa's inflation rate climbed to its highest level in 20 months, largely driven by rising fuel costs connected to the ongoing conflict in Iran and its impact on global energy ma...

item.title: Rising fuel costs prompt Canadian delivery companies to add surcharges, affecting consumer prices
item.description: Canadians are facing another wave of price increases as delivery and trucking companies introduce new fuel surcharges in response to the war in Iran and subsequent global energy price hikes. Documents...

item.title: Kenya Faces Local Protests Amid Global Fuel Crisis
item.description: Kenya experienced widespread protests on May 19 after fuel prices shot up due to the ongoing Persian Gulf crisis and disruptions in oil supply through the Strait of Hormuz. The sudden rise in transpor...

item.title: Swans and Collingwood Coaches Differ on Player Interaction During Matches
item.description: During a recent AFL match, Collingwood coach Craig McRae and Sydney Swans coach Dean Cox highlighted differing views on interactions with opposing players. McRae, who has a history of animated boundar...

item.title: Ways to reach a human Safaricom customer care agent
item.description: Safaricom is one of the most popular mobile network providers in Kenya, with over 50 million subscribers relying on its mobile and M-Pesa services. Despite its wide usage, there isn’t a dedicated phon...

item.title: Tara Lipinski and Todd Kapostasy: Journey through marriage, infertility, and parenthood
item.description: Tara Lipinski, Olympic figure skating champion, and Todd Kapostasy, a producer, have been sharing their journey of love and family life publicly. They first met in 2015 at the Sports Emmy Awards in Ne...
